results.description: At least 60% of consumable.ai_exposure rows must have observed_exposure_pct populated after the LEFT JOIN against base.anthropic_observed_exposure. Coverage is bounded by SOC overlap: Anthropic covers

Among rows where observed_exposure_pct is populated, at least 85% must also have automation_pct populated. This is NOT 100% because SOCs whose contributing Bronze tasks were all fully filtered (filter

If any Anthropic-sourced column (observed_exposure_pct, automation_pct, anthropic_task_count) is populated, anthropic_source_release must also be populated. This enforces provenance integrity: every r
observed_exposure_pct carries through from Silver in 0-100 percent units. A value outside [0, 100] indicates a Gold transformation corrupted the column or the Silver input was out of range.

automation_pct carries through from Silver in 0-100 percent units, consistent with observed_exposure_pct and every other _pct field in this project. A value outside [0, 100] indicates a Gold transform

Where anthropic_task_count is populated, it must be >= 1 (a row with 0 contributing tasks shouldn't exist in Silver, let alone Gold). A zero/negative value indicates the Silver aggregation produced an
All non-null anthropic_source_release values must equal the pinned release 'release_2025_03_27'. Any other value indicates the pipeline pulled from an unexpected release.

Adding Anthropic columns to consumable.ai_exposure via LEFT JOIN must NOT modify existing stat_res or boss_ai_score values. This regression rule compares current rows to a pre-Anthropic baseline (cons
results.evidence: Baseline snapshot table consumable.ai_exposure_baseline_pre_anthropic does not exist in this environment. Rule documented-inapplicable per its own rationale ('archive if baseline unavailable').
